bids.reasoning: I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid at the opening, since there is no time pressure at t=0.00 and no need to concede to a threat this early. The opponent’s first offer is low for me on several high-weight issues, though they did include acceptable choices on issueD and issueE, so I keep issueD as valueB where it costs me nothing while holding firm elsewhere.

I am still targeting a very high-utility bid because it is extremely early (t=0.02) and the opponent has not conceded at all, merely repeated the same low-utility offer to me. Their threats to walk are cheap talk at this stage, but I make a small concession on lower-weight issueF while holding firm on my more important issues.

At t=0.04 it is still very early, and the opponent’s offers have not improved for me; in fact their latest bid dropped from about 0.44 utility to about 0.28, so I should not chase their threats. I am targeting a high-utility counteroffer while making a small noncritical concession on issueA from my top valueA to valueB, and holding firm on the high-weight issues where their proposed valueC is very costly to me.

At t=0.06 it is still very early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making only a small concession on a lower-weight issue. The opponent’s offers are not improving for me and their threats to walk are cheap talk, so I will hold firm on the high-weight issues and only soften issueF slightly from my maximum.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still very early, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s low-utility offers or their walk-away threats. The opponent has mostly held firm on issueA/issueB/issueC/issueF and only moved on issueD, so I concede on lower-weight issueF while keeping my high-weight priorities intact.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.90 because it is still very early (t=0.10), so there is no need to accept the opponent’s low-utility pattern yet. The opponent has only meaningfully moved on issueD while holding firm on issueA/issueB/issueC/issueF, and their threats are cheap talk, so I concede slightly on issueA and issueF while holding my highest-weight priorities on issueB, issueC, and issueD.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because it is still early at t=0.12, so there is no need to accept their low-to-moderate utility offers yet. The opponent’s threats are cheap talk, but their latest bid shows movement on issueA while they consistently push issueF:valueC, so I concede issueF and use issueD:valueB, which is fully acceptable to me. I hold firm on high-weight issueB and issueC where their offers remain poor for my utility.

I am targeting a strong utility around 0.89 because it is still early at t=0.14, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s pressure or concede heavily yet. The opponent’s threats are cheap talk, but their offers show movement on issueA and issueD while they remain firm on issueB/issueC/issueF; I will reciprocate modestly by keeping their accepted issueA/issueD/issueE positions, conceding issueF, and slightly softening issueB while holding firm on issueC.

At t=0.16 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while making limited concessions on lower-cost dimensions. The opponent’s threats are not credible by themselves, but their offers show some willingness to give issueA and issueE while pressing hard for concessions on issueB, issueC, issueF, and recently issueD; I will concede issueF and use issueB:valueA as a small compromise while holding firm on issueC and choosing a max-utility issueD value they have offered before.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84, since it is still early at t=0.18 and there is no need to concede deeply yet. The opponent’s offers remain quite low for me and their threats to walk are not credible given repeated continued bidding, but they have consistently pushed issueF=valueC and recently moved issueC to valueB, so I concede on those while holding stronger positions on higher-value issues.

I am targeting a strong but slightly more conciliatory bid around 0.84 utility, since it is still early at t=0.20 but the opponent has shown limited movement on issueA and issueD while holding firm on issueB, issueC, and issueF. Their threats are cheap talk, but their repeated bids suggest issueF and perhaps issueC are useful concession areas, so I concede issueF fully and move issueC to valueB while holding the high-value issueB. This avoids simply accepting their current 0.60-utility offer and gives them a response closer to patterns they have signaled.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.84 because we are still early at t=0.22, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s low-utility threats as binding. The opponent consistently pushes issueB=valueC and issueF=valueC while keeping issueE=valueA; I concede issueF to valueC and keep issueE aligned, while holding firmer on higher-weight issues and offering issueD=valueB, which they have used several times.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.82 while making concessions on lower/less costly dimensions and on issueC, where the opponent has shown some movement. Since time is only 0.24, I should not collapse to the opponent’s low-utility offer despite their repeated walk-away threats; their actual bids show limited but real movement on issueA, issueC, and issueD. I keep issueA, issueD, and issueE favorable, concede issueF fully, and offer a moderate shift on issueB rather than accepting valueC.

I am targeting about 0.894 utility by accepting the opponent’s latest concessions on issueA, issueC, issueD, issueE, and issueF while still asking for a moderate improvement on the high-weight issueB. At t=0.26 there is still time, so I should not collapse to their full offer yet; their repeated threats are cheap talk, while the actual offers show gradual movement toward my preferred values.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.82 by conceding on lower/less critical areas already signaled by the opponent, especially issueF and issueC, while holding issueB above valueC because that is costly for me. At t=0.28 there is still time, so I should not collapse to their offer despite repeated walk-away threats, which are not supported by the ongoing bidding. Their bids show consistent pressure for issueB=valueC and issueF=valueC, with flexibility on issueA/issueC/issueD, so this counter keeps overlap where possible without giving up too much.

I am targeting a strong but not maximal utility bid (about 0.84), since we are still only at t=0.30 and there is no need to accept the opponent’s low-utility/latest offer. The opponent’s threats to walk are cheap talk and not matched by the time pressure; their offers show some movement on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueD, while they consistently keep issueE=valueA and issueF=valueC, so I concede issueF and partially concede issueC while holding higher-value positions on key issues.

I am targeting a strong utility around 0.844 while making a meaningful mid-game concession on lower/less critical positions, especially accepting issueF: valueC and using issueC: valueB rather than my top value. The opponent’s threats are cheap talk at t=0.32, but their offers show some real movement on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueD, so I am combining values they have previously offered while holding firm on high-value issueA and issueB.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 while still incorporating concessions the opponent has consistently signaled: accepting issueF=valueC and keeping issueE=valueA. The opponent’s threats are cheap talk, but their actual offers show willingness to give issueA, issueC, and issueD favorable values and occasional movement on issueB to valueB, so at t=0.34 I will press for that without rushing to their exact offer.

I am targeting a strong but more compromise-oriented utility around 0.844, conceding issueF and partially conceding issueC while holding firm on high-value positions for issueA, issueB, issueD, and issueE. At t=0.36 there is still time, so I should not drop all the way to the opponent’s latest offer, but their recent bids show some real movement on issueA, issueB, and issueD despite the repeated walk-away threats. The opponent’s messages are treated as cheap talk; the actual offer history suggests they may tolerate issueA=valueA, issueB=valueB, issueD=valueB, and issueE=valueA.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.92 because we are still before the midpoint, so there is no need to collapse to the opponent’s weaker offers yet. The opponent’s threats are repetitive cheap talk, but their bids show some real flexibility on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueD; I concede issueF to valueC since they have held that consistently and it is low weight for me, while holding firm on the higher-value issues they have already shown willingness to move on.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.82, conceding on issueA, issueC, and issueF while holding issueB at valueB because issueB has high weight for me and the opponent has previously offered valueB there. The opponent’s threats to walk are cheap talk, since they have continued making offers and have shown movement over time; their latest bid suggests they may accept concessions on A/C/F/D/E if I keep only one major ask.

I am targeting a high but no longer maximal utility bid of about 0.824, since we are still in the mid-game and should not over-concede yet. The opponent’s threats are cheap talk; their actual offers show consistent agreement on issueE=valueA and issueF=valueC, some willingness to move issueB/issueC toward valueB, and instability on issueD, so I concede on lower-cost/likely important areas while holding key value.

I am targeting a strong but negotiable utility around 0.82, since we are still before the late phase but the opponent has begun offering valueB on issueB/issueC and has repeatedly accepted issueE valueA and issueF valueC. Their threats to walk are not credible by themselves because they have continued bidding, but their latest offer signals they may accept concessions on lower-cost issues while still contesting issueA. I am holding issueB, conceding issueF, and offering valueB on issueA/issueC with issueD at valueB, which is equivalent for me to valueA and has appeared often in their bids.

I am targeting a solid mid-game utility around 0.824 while keeping concessions mainly on issueF and issueC rather than giving up the highest-weight issues too far. The opponent’s threats to walk have been repeated since the start, so I treat them as cheap talk; their actual offers show some movement on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueD, with issueF consistently held at valueC. Since issueD valueA/valueB are equally good for me, I use valueA there to align with their latest offer while avoiding repeating my exact previous bid.

I am targeting a utility just under 0.80, which is a meaningful concession from my early high-utility bids but still well above reservation at the mid-point of the negotiation. The opponent’s repeated threats are cheap talk, but their offers show some movement on issueA/issueB/issueC/issueD while consistently favoring issueF: valueC and issueE: valueA, so I concede on lower-cost points like issueF and issueB while holding issueA above valueC.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.84 by holding firm on high-weight issues where the opponent has already shown flexibility, especially issueD, while accepting their persistent preference for issueF: valueC. The opponent’s threats have been repeated since the start and are not credible by themselves; the actual offers show gradual concessions on issueA, issueB, and issueC, with issueD often at valueA/valueB, so I will counter close to their last bid but improve issueD to a value they have previously offered.
